question: Does the configured bypass actor create an alternate admission path into main?

evidence.item: merge-proof.yml — GH013 rejection documented in fail-fast branch (lines 447-455): 'NULL — proof registry push blocked by repository ruleset (GH013). Admin action required: add github-actions[bot] as a bypass actor'
governance/merge-legitimacy/merge_proof_registry.jsonl — contains only the seed record from PR #1671; zero github-actions[bot] direct-push entries present
main branch commit history (50 most recent) — all commits carry (#PR_NUMBER) suffix; committer=web-flow in all cases; no github-actions[bot] author or committer observed

current_configuration_state.determination: CONFIRMED — the bypass actor is not currently configured. github-actions[bot] cannot push to main without GH013 rejection. No bypass actor activity is observable in main history.

admission_path_analysis.question: If the bypass were configured, would it create an alternate CODE admission path into main?

workflow_scope_analysis.trigger_constraint: Fires ONLY after a PR has already been merged via the canonical path. Cannot precede or circumvent PR admission.
workflow_scope_analysis.commit_message_scope: proof: append merge proof entry for PR #${PR_NUMBER_FOR_COMMIT}
workflow_scope_analysis.operation_type: append-only — idempotency guard prevents duplicate entries

rationale: The bypass trigger is post-merge only — it cannot front-run the PR admission process or admit code before review
The git commit is scoped to a single append-only governance registry file; no source code, workflow, or policy file is touched
The bypass window opens only after the canonical PR path has already been traversed and merged
The merge-proof.yml workflow does not touch any source file, configuration, or workflow file in its push step
When configured, github-actions[bot] can push a single append to governance/merge-legitimacy/merge_proof_registry.jsonl directly to main, bypassing the PR requirement for that one file
This is intentional: the proof registry append IS itself a post-merge governance action, not a code change requiring review
The append records audit evidence of merges that have already passed all governance checks — blocking it would break the canonical audit trail
governance_file_admission_path_determina.note: Per MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json: permitted_bot_actors is empty; github-actions[bot] is not listed as a permitted merge actor. The bypass creates a direct-push path, not a merge legitimacy path. Direct push by github-actions[bot] produces MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL per MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json. The proof registry append is post-merge governance record-keeping, not a merge admission event.

workflow_modification_escalation_risk.vector: If merge-proof.yml were modified to add additional files to the git commit before pushing, the bypass actor could in theory push arbitrary content
mitigations: governance/runtime/MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json — sco_enforcement.required_for_paths includes .github/workflows/** — workflow mutations require SCO before the workflow is merged
Risk class: workflow files are P3 — changes require sovereignty-review authority (per RISK_CLASSIFICATION_RULES.json)
The escalation path itself must traverse the full canonical PR admission process to take effect
workflow_modification_escalation_risk.determination: The escalation risk is real but bounded. Any modification to merge-proof.yml that would widen the push scope must itself pass P3-class governance review, CODEOWNERS approval, and required status checks before it becomes active. The bypass cannot self-escalate.

DIRECT_LIVE_OBSERVATION: main commit history: 50/50 recent commits entered via PR pathway — zero github-actions[bot] direct-push commits
merge_proof_registry.jsonl: contains only seed record (PR #1671); no runtime proof entries from github-actions[bot] direct push
WORKFLOW_SOURCE_ANALYSIS: merge-proof.yml git add is scoped to governance/merge-legitimacy/merge_proof_registry.jsonl only
merge-proof.yml trigger is pull_request:closed with merged==true — post-merge only
merge-proof.yml fail-fast on GH013 with admin action guidance — confirms bypass not yet active
POLICY_ARTIFACT: MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — permitted_bot_actors: [] — github-actions[bot] not listed as permitted merge actor
MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json — bypass via branch protection does not confer merge legitimacy
NOT_DIRECTLY_OBSERVED: Live ruleset GET confirming bypass_actors list (no mcp__github__get_ruleset tool available)
Admin bypass configuration from live GitHub API

formal_determination.evidence_basis: Bypass not yet configured; GH013 blocks all github-actions[bot] direct pushes; zero direct push commits observable in main history
Workflow trigger is post-merge only; git add scoped to proof registry file; cannot front-run or circumvent PR admission
formal_determination.legitimacy_status: MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL — direct push does not create merge legitimacy
formal_determination.escalation_mitigation: CODEOWNERS + P3 risk classification + required status checks on workflow modifications
formal_determination.answer: The configured bypass actor (github-actions[bot]) does NOT create an alternate CODE admission path into main. It creates a narrow, post-merge, append-only direct-push path for the proof registry governance file only, which is the intended design. The bypass is not currently active. When configured, it cannot be used to introduce code changes into main because its admission window opens only after the canonical PR path has been traversed, and its git commit scope is limited to a single governance file.
remaining_open_items: bypass_actor_not_yet_configured — admin must apply PATCH to ruleset 16177949 to activate proof registry bypass
live_ruleset_GET_not_performed — mcp__github__get_ruleset tool not available; bypass_actors list not confirmed empty for non-proof-registry actors
workflow_modification_escalation — residual risk; bounded by CODEOWNERS + P3 governance; classified BOUNDED not CLOSED

impact_on_1604_closure.bypass_actor_verification_gap: RESOLVED — bypass actor is not configured; when configured, it does not create an alternate code admission path
impact_on_1604_closure.direct_push_to_main_pathway: PARTIAL / SUBSTANTIALLY_CONTAINED — unchanged; admin direct push capability remains UNVERIFIED via live ruleset GET
impact_on_1604_closure.1604_closure_eligibility: ENFORCEMENT_CLOSURE_NOT_YET_ELIGIBLE — CI-001 through CI-005 child issues and Phase 1 dependency remain; this analysis resolves the bypass_actor_verification remaining gap from DIRECT_PUSH_AUDIT_REPORT_1604.json v3

tools_used: Read (local — merge-proof.yml, BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json, MERGE_ACTOR_REGISTRY.json, MERGE_BYPASS_INVENTORY.json, DIRECT_PUSH_AUDIT_REPORT_1604.json)
